2603.24307.title: Samasāmayik: A Parallel Dataset for Hindi-Sanskrit Machine Translation
2603.24307.paper_summary: We release Samasāmayik, a novel, meticulously curated, large-scale Hindi-Sanskrit corpus, comprising 92,196 parallel sentences. Unlike most data available in Sanskrit, which focuses on classical era text and poetry, this corpus aggregates data from diverse sources covering contemporary materials, including spoken tutorials, children's magazines, radio conversations, and instruction materials. We benchmark this new dataset by fine-tuning three complementary models - ByT5, NLLB and IndicTrans-v2, to demonstrate its utility. Our experiments demonstrate that models trained on the Samasamayik corpus achieve significant performance gains on in-domain test data, while achieving comparable performance on other widely used test sets, establishing a strong new performance baseline for contemporary Hindi-Sanskrit translation. Furthermore, a comparative analysis against existing corpora reveals minimal semantic and lexical overlap, confirming the novelty and non-redundancy of our dataset as a robust new resource for low-resource Indic language MT.
2603.24307.paper_summary_zh: 我們發布了Samasāmayik，一個新穎、精心策劃的大型印地語-梵語語料庫，包含92,196個平行句子。與大多數可用的梵語數據不同，這些數據主要集中於古典時期的文本和詩歌，這個語料庫匯集了來自多種來源的數據，涵蓋當代材料，包括口語教程、兒童雜誌、廣播對話和教學材料。我們通過微調三個互補模型 - ByT5、NLLB和IndicTrans-v2，來基準測試這個新數據集，以展示其實用性。我們的實驗顯示，基於Samasamayik語料庫訓練的模型在領域內測試數據上取得了顯著的性能提升，同時在其他廣泛使用的測試集上達到了可比的性能，為當代印地語-梵語翻譯建立了一個強大的新性能基準。此外，與現有語料庫的比較分析顯示語義和詞彙重疊極少，確認了我們的數據集作為一個強大的新資源在低資源印地語言機器翻譯中的新穎性和非冗餘性。
2603.24307.author: N J Karthika et.al.
2603.24307.authors: N J Karthika, Keerthana Suryanarayanan, Jahanvi Purohit, Ganesh Ramakrishnan, Jitin Singla, Anil Kumar Gourishetty
